detectors.description: DrainMe.unlockSecret() (DrainMe.sol#157-177) uses a weak PRNG: "secret % 5 == 0 (DrainMe.sol#163)"

DrainMe.unlockSecret() (DrainMe.sol#157-177) uses a dangerous strict equality:
	- secret % 5 == 0 (DrainMe.sol#163)
DrainMe.winPrize() (DrainMe.sol#245-249) ignores return value by owner.call.value(1)() (DrainMe.sol#247)
DrainMe.callFirstTarget() (DrainMe.sol#181-187) ignores return value by firstTarget.call.value(msg.value)() (DrainMe.sol#185)
DrainMe.callSecondTarget() (DrainMe.sol#191-197) ignores return value by secondTarget.call.value(msg.value)() (DrainMe.sol#195)

DrainMe.unlockSecret().secret (DrainMe.sol#161) shadows:
	- DrainMe.secret (DrainMe.sol#39) (state variable)

DrainMe.setSecret(uint256) (DrainMe.sol#105-109) should emit an event for: 
	- secret = _secret (DrainMe.sol#107)
solc-0.4.26 is an outdated solc version. Use a more recent version (at least 0.8.0), if possible.

detectors.description:
Version constraint ^0.4.23 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- DirtyBytesArrayToStorage
	- ABIDecodeTwoDimensionalArrayMemory
	- KeccakCaching
	- EmptyByteArrayCopy
	- DynamicArrayCleanup
	- ImplicitConstructorCallvalueCheck
	- TupleAssignmentMultiStackSlotComponents
	- MemoryArrayCreationOverflow
	- privateCanBeOverridden
	- SignedArrayStorageCopy
	- ABIEncoderV2StorageArrayWithMultiSlotElement
	- DynamicConstructorArgumentsClippedABIV2
	- UninitializedFunctionPointerInConstructor_0.4.x
	- IncorrectEventSignatureInLibraries_0.4.x
	- ABIEncoderV2PackedStorage_0.4.x
	- ExpExponentCleanup
	- EventStructWrongData.
It is used by:
	- ^0.4.23 (DrainMe.sol#11)
